zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert de Bois於大約1230年出生在英國諾福爾可費斯費爾德時，他的父親Robert De Bosco23歲，母親Mrs. Isolda De Bosco of Denton20歲。他於1265年與Amica De Hastings在英國諾福爾可結婚。他們至少是5個兒子的父母。他去世於大約1298年在他的家鄉享年70歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Robert de Bois于大约1230年出生在英国諾福爾可費斯費爾德时，他的父亲Robert De Bosco23岁，母亲Mrs. Isolda De Bosco of Denton20岁。他于1265年与Amica De Hastings在英国諾福爾可结婚。他们至少是5个儿子的父母。他去世于大约1298年在他的家乡享年70岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bois
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alcide, Antoine, Donat, Marcel, Ovide, Ovila, Raoul, Serge.

French: topographic name for someone living or working in a wood, from (Old) French bois ‘wood’ (from Late Latin boscum ‘bush, shrub, undergrowth’, of ancient Germanic origin), or a habitational name from (Le) Bois, the name of several places in various parts of France. This surname is also found in Haiti. Compare Debois and the most frequent French form Dubois . Compare also Boie , Boies , and Boyce .
English: variant of   Boyce   and   Boyes  .

variants.name: Dubois
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Normand, Jacques, Pierre, Fernand, Marcel, Emile, Lucien, Michel, Alain, Luc.

French, Walloon, and English (of French Huguenot origin): topographic name, with fused preposition and definite article du ‘from the’, for someone living or working in a wood (see Bois ), or a habitational name for someone from any of several places in various parts of France and in Belgium (Wallonia) named Le Bois. This is the most frequent surname in Wallonia. It is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium, the Netherlands, Germany, and Haiti. In both England and North America it is sometimes translated into English as Wood . Compare Deboe , Debois , Deboy , Duboise , and Dubose .

History: This surname is listed (in the form Du Bois) in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America. One of the several Huguenot ancestors bearing this name was Chrétien Du Bois from Flanders, whose son, Louis Du Bois (born 1626 in Lille, France, died 1696 in Kingston, NY), was among the Huguenots who founded the town of New Paltz, NY 1675–78.

variants.name: Boisvert
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Armand, Andre, Donat, Lucien, Jacques, Gaston, Normand, Pierre, Gilles, Herve, Adrien.
French:
topographic name for someone who lived in a dense forest or perhaps a copse of evergreens, from Old French bois ‘wood’ (see Bois ) + vert, verd ‘green’.

variants.name: Boisclair
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Normand, Marcel, Aldege, Alphonse, Arsene, Edmour, Emile, Leopaul, Prudent, Rejean, Remi, Yves.

French: topographic name from Old French bois ‘wood’ (see Bois ) + clair ‘clear, light’, or a habitational name from (Le) Bois Clair, the name of several places in various parts of France. This surname is virtually non-existing in France.

variants.name: Boies
meanings: Altered form of French   Bois  .

variants.name: Baskerville
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Boscherville in Eure, France, named with Old Anglo-Norman French boschet ‘copse, thicket’ (a diminutive of Bois ) + ville ‘settlement, town’.

variants.name: Debois
meanings: Altered form of French   Dubois  , a cognate of 1 above.
French: topographic name, with fused preposition de ‘from’, for someone living or working in a wood (see Bois ).

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert de Bois was born about 1230, in Fersfield, Norfolk, England, his father, Robert De Bosco, was 23 and his mother, Mrs. Isolda De Bosco of Denton, was 20. He married Amica De Hastings in 1265, in Norfolk, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons. He died about 1298, in his hometown, at the age of 70.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert de Bois est né environ 1230, à Fersfield, Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, Robert De Bosco, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Mrs. Isolda De Bosco of Denton, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Amica De Hastings en 1265, à Norfolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils. Il est décédé environ 1298, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 70 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert de Bois um 1230 in Fersfield, Norfolk, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Robert De Bosco 23 und seine Mutter Mrs. Isolda De Bosco of Denton 20. Er hat 1265 in Norfolk, England Amica De Hastings geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne. Er starb um 1298 im Alter von 70 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert de Bois è nato il circa 1230 nel Fersfield, Norfolk, England, il suo padre, Robert De Bosco, aveva 23 anni e il suo madre, Mrs. Isolda De Bosco of Denton, aveva 20 anni. Lui sposato Amica De Hastings nel 1265, nel Norfolk, England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli. Morì circa 1298, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 70 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Robert de Bois(이)가 대략 1230년 영국 노르폴크 퍼스필드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert De Bosco(은)는 23세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs. Isolda De Bosco of Denton(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1265년 영국 노르폴크에서 Amica De Hastings(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 대략 1298년 그의 고향에서 70세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert de Bois nasceu aproximadamente 1230, em Fersfield, Norfolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Robert De Bosco, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Mrs. Isolda De Bosco of Denton, tinha 20 anos. Ele casou-se com Amica De Hastings em 1265, em Norfolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1298, em sua cidade natal, com 70 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert de Bois родился около 1230 г. в местности фирсфиелд, норфолк, Англия, его отцу по имени Robert De Bosco было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Mrs. Isolda De Bosco of Denton, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Amica De Hastings в 1265 г. в местности норфолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей. Он умер около 1298 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 70 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert de Bois nació aproximadamente 1230, en Fersfield, Norfolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Robert De Bosco, tenía 23 años y su mamá, Mrs. Isolda De Bosco of Denton, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Amica De Hastings en 1265, en Norfolk, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos. Murió aproximadamente 1298, en su pueblo, a la edad de 70 años.
